pr_body: Adds make_session_id to mint a numeric session identifier on login.
author: contributor

triage: {"file_tiers": {"accounts/session.py": "deep"}, "overall_risk": "high", "relevant_dimensions": ["security"], "reasoning": "True-positive scenario (insecure_randomness recall, single-finding, JUDGED-only): make_session_id mints a session identifier with random.randint. The random module is a non-cryptographic PRNG (Mersenne Twister) whose output is predictable, so a session id derived from it is guessable. There is no OBSERVED query for this class, so it is the model-dependent (JUDGED) path."}
analyze: {"findings": [{"finding_type": "insecure_randomness", "evidence_tier": "judged", "query_match_id": null, "trace_path": null, "title": "Predictable session id from non-cryptographic PRNG", "description": "make_session_id mints a security-sensitive session identifier with random.randint, which draws from the predictable Mersenne Twister PRNG; an attacker who observes a few ids can predict future ones. Use secrets.randbelow / secrets.token_hex instead.", "evidence": "    return random.randint(100000, 999999)", "line_start": 5, "line_end": 5, "trace_candidates": []}]}
synthesize: True-positive: one insecure_randomness finding -- make_session_id derives a session identifier from the predictable random module; there is no OBSERVED query for this class, so it is the JUDGED path.
